subject: Set executable permission on scripts/*
commit_message:
Set executable permission on scripts/*

Those scripts are meant to be executable, hence changing
their permission with u+x

Change-Id: I7e2b286b434fc915ff99146ae834417688ab6dde
Signed-off-by: Alexis de Talhouët <adetalhouet89@gmail.com>

Patch Set 2:

Hi,
First, when I clone the repo I see that it has execution (+x).
NOTE#1: the only 2 scripts that are in use during deployment are: docker_run.sh and docker_health.sh
all others are internal for DEV and QA.
NOTE#2: we have a deployment Jenkins job that copies these scripts and add them +x accordingly. 
thanks.

Patch Set 2:

> First, when I clone the repo I see that it has execution (+x). 

I've done a fresh clone, and I see the scripts has only the rw permission

adetalhouet at bell in ~/temp/sdc/sdc-os-chef/scripts on master
$ ll
total 72
-rw-r--r--  1 adetalhouet  staff   119B  9 Mar 10:16 docker_clean.sh
-rw-r--r--  1 adetalhouet  staff   537B  9 Mar 10:16 docker_health.sh
-rw-r--r--  1 adetalhouet  staff   217B  9 Mar 10:16 docker_login.sh
-rw-r--r--  1 adetalhouet  staff   5.0K  9 Mar 10:16 docker_run.sh
-rw-r--r--  1 adetalhouet  staff   983B  9 Mar 10:16 docker_stats.sh
-rw-r--r--  1 adetalhouet  staff   1.9K  9 Mar 10:16 docker_watchdog.sh
-rw-r--r--  1 adetalhouet  staff   4.4K  9 Mar 10:16 restart_docker.sh

> NOTE#1: the only 2 scripts that are in use during deployment are: docker_run.sh and docker_health.sh all others are internal for DEV and QA. 

I agree, but that doesn't mean they shouldn't have the exec bits.


> NOTE#2: we have a deployment Jenkins job that copies these scripts and add them +x accordingly. thanks.

I understand Jenkins does that, but a developer will not rely on Jenkins for his local development, hence I think those scripts should hold the right permissions from the begining.

Patch Set 3:

Hello Moti,

> as I can't see any difference in your commit.

By looking at the difference between the files in that gerrit, we can see that the only difference is the file mode according to git (shown on the top left of a diff, i.e. https://gerrit.openecomp.org/r/#/c/1825/3/sdc-os-chef/scripts/docker_run.sh):
old mode 100644 --> non executable file
new mode 100755 --> executable file (x)
based on this http://stackoverflow.com/questions/22257640/git-diff-says-old-mode-is-100644-and-new-mode-is-100755-but-nops#comment33805778_22257640

This patch is freshly rebased on master, which means the remote branch does not hold the (x) permission on those scripts.

I can't explain why you have them by default in your environnement, but it seems it might no be reflecting upstream master branch.

Thanks

Patch Set 3:

thank you for your feedback, can you please share how do you modify file permissions ? we could not see that option via WINDOWS? Are you using Linux for your commits? thanks
Change has been successfully merged by Michael Lando

Patch Set 3:

>  can you please share how do you modify file permissions ? 

using the chmod binary: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chmod

i.e. chmod u+x to provide the user to execute.

> we could not see that option via WINDOWS? 

I think there is an equivalent of chmod command for windows, probably attrib or icacls but I don't know how to use those.


> Are you using Linux for your commits?

I'm using both Linux and macOS.
